code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about a sports team (or related entities) into a readable sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Build a graph of relationships
    subject_info = {}  # subject -> list of (predicate, object)
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_info:
            subject_info[t.subject] = []
        subject_info[t.subject].append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # Find the main subject (first triple's subject)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject if triples else ""

    # Define templates for predicates
    templates = {
        "numberOfMembers": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {o} members",
        "season": lambda s, o: f"{s} played in the {o} season",
        "league": lambda s, o: f"{s} competes in the {o} league",
        "manager": lambda s, o: f"{s} is managed by {o}",
        "ground": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays at {o}",
        "fullName": lambda s, o: f"The full name of {s} is {o}",
        "chairman": lambda s, o: f"The chairman of {s} is {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "nickname": lambda s, o: f"{s}, nicknamed {o},",
        "chairmanTitle": lambda s, o: f"The {o} of {s}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "part": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes {o}",
        "mayor": lambda s, o: f"The mayor of {s} is {o}",
        "champions": lambda s, o: f"The champions of {s} are {o}",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {o}",
        "club": lambda s, o: f"{s} plays for {o}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"{s}, whose capital is {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses {o}",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"people from {s} are called {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"{s} speaks {o}",
        "leaderParty": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s leader party is {o}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s official language is {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "youthclub": lambda s, o: f"{s} started at youth club {o}",
        "operator": lambda s, o: f"{s} is operated by {o}",
        "tenant": lambda s, o: f"{s} is tenant of {o}",
        "city": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the city of {o}",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the state of {o}",
    }

    # Build the base sentence from main subject
    base_parts = []
    if main_subject in subject_info:
        for pred, obj in subject_info[main_subject]:
            formatter = templates.get(pred)
            if formatter:
                base_parts.append(formatter(main_subject, obj))
            else:
                base_parts.append(f"{main_subject} {pred} {obj}")

    # Collect related information for relative clauses
    relative_clauses = []
    for subj in subject_info:
        if subj != main_subject:
            for pred, obj in subject_info[subj]:
                if pred == "city":
                    relative_clauses.append(f"which is in the city of {obj}")
                elif pred == "state":
                    relative_clauses.append(f"which is in the state of {obj}")
                elif pred == "country":
                    relative_clauses.append(f"which is in {obj}")
                elif pred == "capital":
                    relative_clauses.append(f"where the capital is {obj}")
                elif pred == "isPartOf":
                    relative_clauses.append(f"which is part of {obj}")
                elif pred == "leader":
                    relative_clauses.append(f"whose leader is {obj}")
                elif pred == "mayor":
                    relative_clauses.append(f"whose mayor is {obj}")
                elif pred == "operator":
                    relative_clauses.append(f"which is operated by {obj}")

    # Join all parts into a single fluent sentence
    sentence = ", ".join(base_parts + relative_clauses) + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
